result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/3/patron-flesh/cli-flag-edit.md`.

Key calls I made as the patron:

- **Framed as extension, not new verb.** `nsg writ edit --title` beats a new `nsg writ rename` (#3, #26).
- **Scoped the "set" deliberately.** The editable free-text fields on a writ are `{title, body}`; adding `--title` *completes* the set rather than opening a general field-editor (#36). Explicitly ruled out status/type/parent edits — those have their own lifecycles.
- **Fail-loud on empty flags and empty values** (#2). No silent no-op when neither flag is passed.
- **Atomic apply** when both flags are present (#4) — one write, one event.
- **Fix the source, not the consumer** (#31): if `--body` today is a hard-coded path rather than a general writ-update, fix that first and route both flags through the same function.
- **Cut audit/history, bulk ops, interactive prompt** as second-consumer speculation (#18, #23).
- **Named the reader**: a one-off corrective use by an author/operator who realized the title is wrong (#22).

Deferred three questions to the Distiller: title validation rules on create, whether `--body` supports stdin/`@file` (and whether `--title` should mirror), and whether any writ type has an immutable title.
